questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following statements concerning quicklime is INCORRECT?

options.label: A
options.text: Heating marble strongly can form quicklime.

options.label: B
options.text: Reacting quicklime with sulphur dioxide can form calcium sulphate.

options.label: C
options.text: Reacting quicklime with carbon dioxide can form calcium carbonate.

options.label: D
options.text: A large amount of heat evolves when quicklime is put into water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following statements concerning quartz is correct?

options.label: C
options.text: Quartz conducts electricity by delocalised electrons.

options.label: D
options.text: Quartz is hard because it has a giant covalent network structure.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
What is the mass of oxygen in 24.0 g of  \( CuSO_{4} \cdot 5H_{2}O(s) \) ?

(Relative atomic masses : H = 1.0, O = 16.0, S = 32.1, Cu = 63.5)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following statements concerning francium (atomic number = 87) is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Francium has a higher melting point than potassium.

options.label: B
options.text: Francium forms cations more readily than potassium.

options.label: C
options.text: Francium is a weaker oxidising agent than potassium.

options.label: D
options.text: Francium has a fewer number of occupied electron shells than potassium.

questions.question_text:
Refer to the following chemical cell :
[IMG:q9_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following statements is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Copper is the cathode of the cell.

options.label: B
options.text: Zinc ions act as the oxidising agent in the cell.

options.label: C
options.text: Only zinc ions can pass through the porous pot.

options.label: D
options.text: Electrons flow from copper to zinc through the external circuit.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
A reaction occurs when water is dropped into the mixture in the set-up below. A colourless gas is given out.
[IMG:q11_stem_0.jpg]

What is the role of water in this reaction?

options.label: A
options.text: Water reacts with sodium carbonate to give the colourless gas.

options.label: B
options.text: Water reacts with citric acid to give the colourless gas.

options.label: C
options.text: Water is a medium for the formation of carbonate ions from sodium carbonate.

options.label: D
options.text: Water is a medium for the formation of hydrogen ions from citric acid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
Refer to the following half equations :

\(\begin{array}{l}\mathrm{C}_{2}\mathrm{O}_{4}^{2-}(\mathrm{aq})\rightarrow2\mathrm{CO}_{2}(\mathrm{g})+2\mathrm{e}^{-}\\\mathrm{MnO}_{4}^{-}(\mathrm{aq})+8\mathrm{H}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})+5\mathrm{e}^{-}\rightarrow\mathrm{Mn}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq})+4\mathrm{H}_{2}\mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})\end{array} \)

What is the minimum volume of 0.010 M acidified  \( KMnO_{4}(aq) \)  required to completely oxidise 15.00 cm\( ^{3} \) of 0.020 M  \( Na_{2}C_{2}O_{4}(aq) \) ?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The enthalpy changes for some conversions are shown below :
\( \mathrm{W}\xrightarrow{\Delta H=-150\mathrm{kJ}\ mol^{-1}}\mathrm{X}\xrightarrow{\Delta H=+100\mathrm{kJ}\ mol^{-1}}\mathrm{Y}\xrightarrow{\Delta H=+60\mathrm{kJ}\ mol^{-1}}\mathrm{Z} \)

Which of the following combinations is correct?
\(\mathbf{W}\xrightarrow{\quad}\mathbf{Z} \)    |    \(\mathbf{Z}\xrightarrow{\quad}\mathbf{X} \)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The observations of heating three metal carbonates are shown below :
Metal carbonateObservation
\( X_{2}CO_{3} \)A gas was given out and a shiny silvery solid was formed.
\( Y_{2}CO_{3} \)There was no observable change.
\( ZCO_{3} \)A gas was given out and a yellow solid was formed.
Which of the following shows the decreasing order of reactivity of the metals?

questions.question_text:

[IMG:q16_stem_0.jpg]

What is the volume of the reagent used in the titration?

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
Which of the following ways is / are acceptable in the storage of the chemical concerned?

(1) Store concentrated  \( H_{2}SO_{4}(l) \)  in a copper container.
(2) Store concentrated  \( AgNO_{3}(aq) \)  in a brown glass container.
(3) Store concentrated  \( \mathrm{Pb(NO_{3})_{2}(aq)} \)  in an iron container.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following steps can be involved in preparing copper(II) chloride crystals?

(1) Add  \( CuCO_{3}(s) \)  to HCl(aq).
(2) Add  \( \mathrm{Cu(NO_{3})_{2}(s)} \)  to NaCl(aq).
(3) Add  \( \mathrm{Cu(s)} \)  to HCl(aq).

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
Which of the following processes can form a halogen?

(1) Electrolyse concentrated KCl(aq).
(2) Add  \( Na_{2}SO_{4}(s) \)  to concentrated HBr(aq).
(3) Add KI(s) to acidified  \( KMnO_{4}(aq) \) .

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Refer to the sketch below :
[IMG:q20_stem_0.jpg]

Which of the following can explain the variation of the boiling points of the hydrogen halides?

(1) The boiling point of HF is higher than that of HCl because the hydrogen bonds between HF molecules are stronger than the van der Waals' forces between HCl molecules.
(2) The boiling point of HI is higher than that of HBr because HI molecules are more polar than HBr molecules.
(3) HCl has the lowest boiling point because it has the smallest molecular size.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements are correct?

(1) The standard enthalpy change of formation of  \( NH_{3}(g) \)  can be determined directly from experiment.
(2) The standard enthalpy change of combustion of  \( H_{2}NNH_{2}(l) \)  is negative.
(3) The standard enthalpy change of formation of  \( N_{2}(g) \)  is zero.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements concerning ice and water at  \(0^{\circ}\) C are correct?

(1) The density of ice is lower than that of water because ice has an open structure but water does not.
(2) In ice, the hydrogen bonds between the molecules are weaker than the covalent bonds in the molecules.
(3) In ice, each molecule links up with only two neighbouring molecules by hydrogen bonds.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following hazard warning labels should be displayed on a bottle containing methanol?

(1) [IMG:q23_stem_0.jpg] (2) [IMG:q23_stem_1.jpg] (3) [IMG:q23_stem_2.jpg]

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
1st statement : Perspex can be used to make shopping bags.
2nd statement : Perspex is a condensation polymer.

options.label: A
options.text: Both statements are true and the 2nd statement is a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: B
options.text: Both statements are true but the 2nd statement is NOT a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: C
options.text: The 1st statement is false but the 2nd statement is true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following reaction :
\( 4H_{2}(g)+Fe_{3}O_{4}(s)\rightarrow3Fe(s)+4H_{2}O(l) \)

What is the minimum volume of  \( H_{2}(g) \)  at room conditions required to form 0.168 g of Fe(s)?

(Molar volume of gas at room conditions = 24 dm \( ^{3} \) ; 
Relative atomic mass : Fe = 55.8)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the information below :

Reaction    |    Equilibrium constant at  \( 25^{\circ} \) C
\( H_{2}A(aq) \rightleftharpoons H^{+}(aq) + HA^{-}(aq) \)    |    \( 1.3 \times 10^{-3} \)  mol dm \( ^{-3} \)
\( HA^{-}(aq) \rightleftharpoons H^{+}(aq) + A^{2-}(aq) \)    |    \( 3.1 \times 10^{-6} \)  mol dm \( ^{-3} \)
\( 2HA^{-}(aq) \rightleftharpoons H_{2}A(aq) + A^{2-}(aq) \)    |    X

What is the numerical value of X?

questions.question_text: Which of the following alkanols can form a ketone by warming with acidified sodium dichromate solution?

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 12: Patterns in the Chemical World
questions.question_text: Which of the following statements concerning the oxides of elements in the third period of the Periodic Table is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: \( SiO_{2}(s) \)  dissolves in water to form a neutral solution.

options.label: B
options.text: \( P_{4}O_{10}(s) \)  dissolves in water to form an acidic solution.

options.label: C
options.text: \( Al_{2}O_{3}(s) \)  dissolves in water to form an alkaline solution.

options.label: D
options.text: \( Cl_{2}O(g) \)  dissolves in water to form  \( Cl_{2}(aq) \)  and  \( O_{2}(g) \)  only.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Refer to the following chemical reaction :
\( \mathrm{H_{2}O(l)\rightleftharpoons H^{+}(aq)+OH^{-}(aq)}\quad\Delta H>0 \)

The pH of a pure water sample is 7.0 at  \( 25^{\circ} \) C. Which of the following statements is / are correct when the sample has been heated to  \( 50^{\circ} \)C?

(1) The  \( [OH^{-}(aq)] \)  of the sample is  \( 1.0 \times 10^{-7} \)  mol dm \( ^{-3} \) .
(2) The pH of the sample is smaller than 7.0.
(3) The sample remains neutral.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements concerning nylon-6,6 are correct?

(1) Fishing net can be made from nylon-6,6.
(2)  \( H_{2}N(CH_{2})_{6}NH_{2} \)  is one of the monomers of nylon-6,6.
(3) The intermolecular attractions in nylon-6,6 are covalent bonds.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Refer to the following set-up :
[IMG:q35_stem_0.jpg]

(1) \(MgO(s) + 2HCl(aq) → MgCl_{2}(aq) + H_{2}O(l)\)
(2)  \( Na_{2}S_{2}O_{3}(aq) + 2HCl(aq) \rightarrow S(s) + SO_{2}(g) + H_{2}O(l) + 2NaCl(aq) \)
(3)  \( \mathrm{Mg(s) + ZnSO_{4}(aq) \rightarrow MgSO_{4}(aq) + Zn(s)} \)

Which of the following reactions can the effect of concentration on rate be studied by the above set-up?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
1st statement : The rate of conversion from glucose to ethanol is increased by adding yeast.
2nd statement : The conversion from glucose to ethanol is catalysed by enzymes in yeast.

options.label: A
options.text: Both statements are true and the 2nd statement is a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: B
options.text: Both statements are true but the 2nd statement is NOT a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: C
options.text: The 1st statement is false but the 2nd statement is true.
